title: China’s Shijian-31 satellite is sweeping GEO using a unique orbit
summary: China's Shijian-31 satellite, launched in June, is operating in a unique orbit that provides a distinctive view of the geostationary belt. This classified satellite's creative orbital path sets it apart from typical geostationary satellites.

title: FCC approves sweeping space reforms while clearing more C-band for 5G
summary: The FCC approved significant space industry reforms on July 22, including pushing satellite operators out of additional C-band spectrum for a 5G auction next year. The agency also implemented a licensing overhaul to accelerate the commercial space economy.

title: Vast establishes division for national security projects
summary: Vast has created a new division focused on national security space projects. This move aims to expand the company's role in government and defense-related space initiatives.

title: The strategic competition with China: winning the multi-domain race
summary: The article discusses the strategic competition with China, emphasizing the need to win the multi-domain race in military innovation. It highlights how historical military advancements have reshaped warfare and rendered past strategies obsolete.

title: Relativity Space to expand Terran R production in Florida
summary: Relativity Space has partnered with Space Florida to establish manufacturing and test facilities for its Terran R rocket on Florida’s Space Coast. This expansion aims to boost production capabilities for the company's next-generation launch vehicle.

title: Artemis 2 astronaut advocates for equatorial lunar landings
summary: The pilot of NASA's Artemis 2 mission suggests prioritizing human landings in the moon's equatorial regions over the challenging south pole for the initial return to the lunar surface. This recommendation aims to simplify early Artemis missions while still advancing lunar exploration.

title: Gravity-1 sea launch off Shanghai puts 9 satellites into orbit
summary: Orienspace successfully launched its third Gravity-1 solid rocket from waters off Shanghai, deploying nine satellites into orbit. The 30-meter-long rocket marks another milestone for the commercial launch provider.

title: Zenno Astronautics to relocate to the United States
summary: Zenno Astronautics, a New Zealand startup specializing in superconducting magnets for spacecraft applications, will relocate its headquarters to the United States. The move aims to enhance its operational and strategic positioning in the growing space industry.

title: SpaceX launches Northrop mission to extend the life of aging satellites
summary: SpaceX launched a Northrop Grumman mission to extend the life of aging satellites by installing propulsion pods on three commercial satellites in geostationary orbit. The DARPA-backed vehicle aims to enhance satellite longevity through in-orbit servicing.

title: Northrop adds to charges on Vulcan solid rocket motor program
summary: Northrop Grumman incurred additional charges for the solid rocket boosters used in the Vulcan Centaur program and indicated that updated motors may not be ready until the end of the year.

title: China's record-breaking Gravity-1 rocket launches 9 satellites from a ship at sea (video)
summary: China's Gravity-1 rocket, the world's most powerful solid rocket, successfully launched nine satellites into orbit from a ship at sea. The mission marked the third flight for Orienspace's Gravity-1.

title: After nearly 30 years, NASA realized this near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet. The discovery may help us defend the planet some day
summary: NASA discovered that a near-Earth asteroid is actually a comet after nearly 30 years. This finding could aid in identifying more hidden comets and enhancing planetary defense.

title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope could reveal black holes ripping up stars. It's set to launch Aug. 30
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, launching on August 30, may reveal black holes disrupting stars. It could uncover hidden black hole activity even during cosmic noon.

title: If space war erupts, 'none of us will be able to avoid the war zone,' warns outgoing Space Force chief in final public speech
summary: The outgoing U.S. Space Force chief warned in his final speech that a space war would affect everyone, as no one could avoid the conflict. He emphasized the global impact of potential space warfare.

title: 'Terminator 2: Judgment Day' is time-travelling back into theaters next month for a 35th anniversary re-release
summary: The classic film 'Terminator 2: Judgment Day' will return to theaters for a 35th anniversary re-release next month. The event celebrates the movie's legacy with a nostalgic 1991-themed promotion.

title: Astronomers may have discovered the 1st moon outside of our solar system — or is it something weirder?
summary: Astronomers may have discovered the first exomoon outside our solar system, or it could be an unusual object challenging planetary and lunar definitions. The finding raises questions about the nature of celestial bodies beyond our solar system.

title: Our 1st-ever up-close look at the Martian surface | Space photo of the day for July 22, 2026
summary: The first-ever close-up photograph of the Martian surface has been captured, showing rocks in black-and-white. This marks a historic milestone in space exploration.

title: 'The court has to intervene now': Indigenous groups and conservationists condemn SpaceX's South Texas land swap
summary: Indigenous groups and conservationists have filed an emergency injunction to block a land swap between SpaceX and the federal government, arguing that courts must determine its legality. The move comes amid concerns over environmental and tribal impacts in SpaceX's South Texas operations.

title: New timelapse video shows NASA's Psyche spacecraft zooming by Mars
summary: A new timelapse video showcases NASA's Psyche spacecraft passing by Mars, complementing existing Mars science with unique data despite limited anticipated discoveries.

title: Soyuz MS-29 crew arrives at ISS, U.S. EVA completes Canadarm2 repairs
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft successfully docked with the International Space Station (ISS), bringing three new crew members aboard. Additionally, a U.S. spacewalk completed repairs on the Canadarm2 robotic arm.

title: Path to Starship Flight 13 requires additional Booster 20 test
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Starship Flight 13, targeting a launch no earlier than July 23, but requires additional testing of Booster 20. The delay highlights ongoing technical challenges in the Starship program.

title: Falcon 9 to launch MRV-1 robotic servicing spacecraft for Northrop Grumman
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is scheduled to launch the MRV-1 robotic servicing spacecraft for Northrop Grumman from Florida. The Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV-1) aims to demonstrate in-space servicing capabilities.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Starship, and Chinese rockets scheduled to launch
summary: SpaceX is preparing for a new launch attempt of Starship Flight 13 after the previous attempt was aborted. Additionally, Falcon 9 and Chinese rockets are also scheduled for upcoming launches.

title: Making Progress: Relativity’s Terran R, Rocket Lab’s Neutron, and PLD Space’s Miura 5
summary: Relativity Space, Rocket Lab, and PLD Space are making progress on their new rocket developments: Terran R, Neutron, and Miura 5, respectively. While Starship's test program has been in the spotlight, these companies continue advancing their launch vehicle projects.

title: Starship Flight 13: Booster 20 Aborts During Ignition
summary: Starship Flight 13's Booster 20 aborted during ignition after a planned launch attempt on July 16 at 5:45 pm CDT. The mission was postponed due to the abort.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for the first land recovery of a booster.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: Soyuz MS-29 successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. This marks another mission to maintain the station's operational crew complement.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is preparing for its 13th test flight. Seven launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key step toward its upcoming mission.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration.

title: Plato's electronics ready for space
summary: ESA's PLATO spacecraft has completed key electromagnetic compatibility tests in the Maxwell Test Chamber, confirming its electronics are fit for space. The mission is now cleared for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in March 2027.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies for space missions.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting industry events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across its programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for versatile applications in the space industry. This innovation aims to enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and set to resume formation flying, enabling the creation of artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The UK Scraps the Department in Charge of UKSA
summary: The UK has decided to abolish the department overseeing the UK Space Agency (UKSA) during the Farnborough Air Show. This move, announced by new Prime Minister Andy Burnham, marks a significant shift in the country's space policy.

title: The UK and Space Florida Launch Joint Fund
summary: The UK and Space Florida have launched a joint fund to support projects with partners from both regions. This initiative aims to foster collaboration between the UK and Florida's space sectors.

title: Breaking Down the $686B Space Economy
summary: The global space economy reached a record $686 billion in 2025, as reported by the Space Foundation. This marks significant growth in the industry's economic impact.

title: FCC Eyes Wi-Fi and Bluetooth Bands for Satellites
summary: The FCC is considering a proposal to permit unlicensed wireless devices like Wi-Fi and Bluetooth to communicate directly with satellites. This move aims to expand satellite connectivity options for consumer devices.

title: deltaVision Raises €10.2M to Build In-Orbit Refueling Tech
summary: deltaVision has raised €10.2 million to develop in-orbit refueling technology. The funds will be used to expand manufacturing capacity and research new capabilities.

title: UK Commits £62M into Domestic Space Capabilities
summary: The UK government allocated £62 million ($83.3 million) to boost domestic satellite communications and space technologies. This investment aims to enhance the UK's space capabilities.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Reaches Orbit with Vikram-1 Rocket
summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched its Vikram-1 rocket, becoming the first private Indian company to reach orbit. The launch took place from India's Satish Dhawan Space Centre.

title: DoD Adds $11.4B to NatSec Launch Contracts
summary: The U.S. Department of Defense increased its national security space launch contract ceiling by $11.4 billion, tripling the total pool from $5.6 billion to $17 billion. This expansion significantly boosts funding for future launch contracts under the National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program.

title: Beyond Reach Labs Raises $10M Seed, Moves to NYC
summary: Beyond Reach Labs has raised $10 million in a seed funding round and announced a move to New York City. The company focuses on addressing size, weight, and power constraints in spaceflight.

title: NASA Selects Starlink for Artemis III Mission
summary: NASA has awarded SpaceX a contract to provide laser communications for the Artemis III mission using Starlink. This marks a significant step in integrating commercial space technologies into NASA's lunar exploration plans.

title: FCC Approves a New C-Band Auction
summary: The FCC approved a new C-band auction to clear 160 megahertz of the upper C-band spectrum from satellite operators. This auction aims to build on previous C-band spectrum reallocation efforts.

title: Lite Coms Wins $22M Contract to Support Operational Readiness
summary: Lite Coms secured a $22 million contract from an unnamed government and defense customer to provide satellite communications for operational readiness. The company specializes in integration and manufacturing of satellite communication solutions.

title: FCC Overhauls Satellite Licensing, Adopts ‘Assembly Line’ Approach
summary: The FCC approved a major overhaul of its satellite and earth station licensing process, introducing a streamlined 'licensing assembly line' approach. This change aims to modernize and expedite the approval process for space and earth station licenses.

title: Iridium Reports 4% Revenue Growth in Q2 Amid Rocket Lab Acquisition Deal
summary: Iridium reported a 4% revenue growth in Q2 while undergoing an acquisition deal with Rocket Lab. The satellite operator's financial performance and acquisition status were highlighted in their latest report.

title: Contrivian Completes Merger With Big Network to Fuel Orchestration Service
summary: Contrivian has completed its merger with Big Network to create a vertically integrated connectivity platform. The merger aims to enhance orchestration services through a multi-modal approach.

title: SpaceX Launches Northrop Grumman’s First Mission Robotic Vehicle
summary: SpaceX successfully launched Northrop Grumman's first Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV-MEP) for on-orbit refueling and satellite life extension. The mission marks a significant step in satellite servicing and refueling technology.

title: Arianespace Sets Date for Fourth Ariane 6 Launch This Year
summary: Arianespace has scheduled the fourth Ariane 6 launch of the year for August 27, aiming to deploy the second Meteosat Third Generation Imager satellite (MTG-I2). This mission highlights Arianespace's ongoing efforts to enhance Earth observation capabilities.

title: Poland Commits Nearly $750M to Europe’s IRIS² Constellation
summary: Poland has committed 656 million euro ($748 million) to Europe's IRIS² satellite constellation, covering nearly 10% of the project's funding. The agreement was signed in Warsaw to support the development of the constellation.

title: UK Government to Invest Further Millions to Boost Space Sector
summary: The UK government is investing an additional £62 million ($83.4 million) to strengthen the country's space sector, focusing on new satellite communications systems. This funding aims to enhance the growth and capabilities of the UK's space industry.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Makes History for India With First Launch Success
summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched its Vikram-1 rocket, marking India's first private orbital launch. The mission achieved orbit, making it a historic milestone for the country's space industry.
